dialogueChunks.intro: It's done, {player_name}. The commander left {location} at dawn with nothing but the clothes on their back.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When the evidence was presented to the whole settlement, even the loyal guards turned against the commander. {target_npc} read the inventory records aloud in the square: every diverted crate, every hidden surplus, every lie. The silence that followed was the loudest thing I've ever heard. The people voted unanimously: the commander is banished, and a new council will govern {location} from now on.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're free to govern ourselves for the first time in years. Take this: it was liberated from the commander's private stockpile. It belongs to the people, and the people want you to have it.

playerResponses.text: A settlement governing itself. That's how it should always have been.
I'm glad the truth came out. Use your freedom wisely.

dialogueChunks.intro: The trading post is open, {player_name}. Officially, permanently, and defiantly open.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The first independent merchants arrived this morning, and the prices were half what the consortium charged. {target_npc} is managing operations, and they're grinning so wide I'm worried their face will crack. The consortium sent a threatening letter, but three other settlements have already declared support for the free market. They can't shut down what they can't surround. We started something here that's going to spread across the region.
dialogueChunks.outro: Free trade. Fair prices. Open roads. You made this possible. Take this with the gratitude of every independent trader in the territory.

playerResponses.text: A free market spreading across the region? That's a revolution worth fighting for.
Good to see the consortium humbled. Keep the post running.

dialogueChunks.intro: The warden is in chains, {player_name}. Actual chains, in the very cell where they held {target_npc}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When the workers testified about what they'd been forced to do, the entire settlement turned. Even the warden's personal guard laid down their weapons. The deal with the {enemy_type} is broken: {target_npc} led a delegation to the border and made it clear that no more labor would be provided. The workers are home with their families for the first time in months, and the warden will face a tribunal in the morning.
dialogueChunks.outro: Justice isn't always clean, but this time it was thorough. Take this: you earned it by standing with people who had no one else to stand with.

playerResponses.text: The workers are home and the warden is in chains. That's the right ending.
Make sure the tribunal is fair. Even tyrants deserve due process.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}. You should see {location} right now. It doesn't even look like the same place.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The tax is abolished. The barracks have been converted into a community hall. {target_npc} was elected as the new settlement representative by unanimous vote: the first time leadership here was actually chosen by the people. The soldiers who stayed accepted the new arrangement, and the ones who didn't left peacefully. There was a feast last night, the first in months where people ate without guilt or fear.
dialogueChunks.outro: We ate freely and laughed freely for the first time since anyone can remember. This is yours: a token of a people who remembered what freedom tastes like.

playerResponses.text: A feast without fear. That's what you were fighting for all along.
Freedom earned by the people themselves. I was just the spark.
